docs.title: libdmmy
docs.text: Hi, you've just entered my cool website. There's not much documentation here yet, but the number will grow over time (I hope).

docs.title: About me
docs.text: libdmmy (Dummy Yenko). Not a big fan of touching grass, but a big fan of coding and Minecraft...

docs.title: Redmi Note 8 (ginkgo)
docs.text:
Main phone. Has ArrowOS 12 installed. Doesn't have any GApps, because it does much useless stuff at the background.

Fun facts

  • I've hard-bricked it when flashed too old version of MIUI and had to repair it through EDL.
  • Instead of shutting down it just reboots on the first time, and adb reboot recovery will actually reboot into system on the first time too.

docs.title: PC (potato)
docs.text:
Main PC with ancient parts. Intel i3-2100, NVIDIA GT440, 8GB of RAM. Works smooth for me, because I haven't tried anything better. However, when it comes to Minecraft, Linux sucks, because of ancient NVIDIA graphics card. But on Windows works just fine (Windows 7 is fastest for it, but it's too old).

Fun fact

After three years, SSD still lives, even after reinstalling OSes every two weeks, because I'm getting bored.

docs.title: Redmi 7A (pine)
docs.text:
Second phone. Using it as a hotspot, a way to use apps for normies (e.g. banking, WhatsApp and other gov. stuff) and just a fallback, when something goes wrong on ginkgo. Yes, I'll take two phones when I need to go somewhere, or I'll just leave my ginkgo at home.

Fun fact

Sometimes it randomly reboots pretending that its EMMC is about to die.

docs.title: markdown
docs.text: to make newline, insert two spaces at the end of desired line

docs.title: useful information
docs.text: there's vd (visidata) utility to view sqlite databases in a nice TUI.

docs.title: basic stuff
docs.text:
create a new database and enter it in command line: 

sqlite3 database.db


-- this is comment btw
-- you can write syntax in either lowercase or uppercase

-- start making changes
begin transaction;

-- without `if not exists` it'll throw an error if table already exists
create table if not exists users (
 -- <NAME> <TYPE> <OPTIONS>,
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    age integer check (age >= 0),
    email text unique
);

-- insert data
-- please note, that strings are accepted only in single-quotes,
--   not " one;
INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES ('dummy', 16);

-- update data
UPDATE users SET age = 17 WHERE name = 'dummy';

-- delete data
DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 101;

-- save changes
commit;

-- view table's columns
pragma table_info(users);
-- 0|id|INTEGER|0||1
-- 1|name|TEXT|1||0
-- 2|age|INTEGER|0||0
-- 3|email|TEXT|0||0

begin transaction;

-- rename table
ALTER TABLE users RENAME TO customers;

-- select data
SELECT * FROM customers WHERE age > 25 ORDER BY name DESC LIMIT 5;

-- add column
ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN phone TEXT;

-- drop column
ALTER TABLE customers DROP COLUMN phone;

-- delete table
DROP TABLE customers;

-- drop changes
rollback;

docs.title: commands
docs.text:
create a new session: 

tmux new
add -s your_session_name if you want to set name of session

Get in that session: 

tmux at
add -t your_session_name if you specified name of session

docs.title: useful keybindings (default)
docs.text: Ctrl+B c - create a new window Ctrl+B d - detach from session, it'll keep running in the background Ctrl+B ? - display available keybindings Ctrl+B n - switch to the next window Ctrl+B p - switch to the previous window Ctrl+B & - kill current window
